channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Fernandes FRB-100 5-String Bass - Refinished Japanese Sen + Bartolini + HSC — $800
item.description:
$800 · Excellent · Jam's Gear

GORGEOUS open grain J style 5-string by Fernandes Japan available!!

This was refinished by Bruce in VA from the stock black gloss finish and we are all better off for it, the grain detail on this Japanese Sen Ash piece is absolutely stunning.

Electronics were upgraded to bartolini Classic J pickups and a Bartolini NTBT preamp. Also added was an aguilar style (MN Taper) blend pot for more effective application of the blend control. Vintage tone control was also added similar to the sadowsky style preamp. Tone control is Push/Pull for Active/Passive.

Gold MIJ hardware is original, and the bridge is a type that is really rare and highly functional/adjustable.

Really well balanced, nice flat radius and setup perfectly with fresh round core GHS nickel strings.

Beautifully polished frets that are hardly worn down, with a hand carved bone nut added for high-end performance.

minimal wear other than normal usage, except for one faint dent and one filled chip on the back of the neck, neither of which are noticeable while playing. illustrated in pics. also a small finish chip around the tuning post of the D string.

Hardshell case included! Soundtown TSA

Cannot ship currently but pickup in NYC is welcome, also willing to drive within reasonable distance of the NYC tristate area if you want to meet.

Will update ASAP if anything changes on the shipping front!

item.title: Fender Kingman Bass SCE Acoustic / Electric Bass 2010s - Sunburst — $375
item.description:
$375 · Very Good · NC Gear

Nice Kingman Bass. Comes with gigbag.

item.title: Hofner Verythin Bass - Black w/ Upgrades — $700
item.description:
$700 · Good · Jam's Gear

Beautiful classic style Hofner with hardware and electronic upgrades available!

This is a player, has plenty of wear from normal usage and a handful of finish scratches, but nothing structural or functional. Has one larger (~1cm) finish chip off the neck pickup. Lightweight but very sturdy. Following mods have been made:

- standard bridge was replaced with an archtop style rosewood bridge (for the more "woody" tone), but I can include the standard hofner bridge ON REQUEST.

- Gretsch-style electronics harness with US spec parts by Bourns and Switchcraft (See photo for description) for more precise control

- Tuning machines were replaced with deluxe Grover Sta-tite 18:1 tuners for smoother function over stock look

Setup with fresh Labella Tapewound strings which covers everything from motown to mr. kite.

No way to ship this at the current moment but I am willing to meet in the NYC tri state area if you are interested, local pickup prioritized.

I will update the ad asap if shipping comes into play!

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5 HH with Maple Fretboard 1999 - 2018 - Dark Cherry Burst — $2,699.99
item.description:
$2,699.99 · Very Good · Afterlight Select

2008 Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay5 HH USA • Black Cherry Burst • Matching Headstock • Maple • Afterlight Select


Some basses get noticed because they’re expensive.

Others get noticed because they stop people in their tracks.

This is one of those basses.

Finished in Black Cherry Burst with a matching painted headstock, paired with a beautifully aged maple neck and fretboard, this 2008 USA StingRay5 HH has the kind of visual presence that’s become increasingly difficult to find. Under bright light, the transparent cherry finish reveals the wood grain beneath, while fading to deep black around the edges. It’s one of those finishes that keeps rewarding a second look.

Then you pick it up.

And you remember why StingRays have been a studio and stage standard for decades.

The dual Music Man humbuckers deliver everything from articulate vintage-inspired tones to aggressive modern punch, while the 5-string platform gives you all the low-end authority you’d expect from one of the most respected bass designs ever built.

This isn’t just another StingRay.

It’s one with personality.


Specifications

  • 2008 Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay5 HH
  • Made in San Luis Obispo, California, USA
  • Black Cherry Burst
  • Matching headstock
  • Maple neck
  • Maple fretboard
  • Dual Music Man humbuckers (HH)
  • Chrome hardware
  • Original Music Man bridge
  • Original Music Man tuners
  • Factory black pickguard
  • Includes factory hard shell case (if applicable—remove if not)


Condition

This bass presents beautifully and has clearly been cared for.

It shows normal signs of honest use expected from a well-loved instrument of this age, including light cosmetic wear consistent with regular playing. Nothing distracting, nothing abusive—just the kind of wear that comes from an instrument that has been enjoyed rather than hidden away.

The finish still has exceptional depth and gloss, the maple neck has aged beautifully, and overall it remains an incredibly striking instrument.


Why This One Hits Different

Music Man has produced countless StingRays.

Very few combine all of these features:

  • Black Cherry Burst
  • Matching headstock
  • Maple fingerboard
  • HH dual-pickup configuration
  • USA craftsmanship
  • 2008 production

The result is a StingRay that clearly looks and feels more special than the average example.

It’s familiar enough to be instantly recognizable.

Different enough that musicians stop and take a second look.


Afterlight Select

At Afterlight Select, we don’t simply list instruments—we curate them.

Every guitar, bass, amplifier, and piece of gear is hand-selected because something about it stands apart. Sometimes it’s exceptional condition. Sometimes it’s a thoughtful upgrade path. Sometimes it’s simply a finish, a feel, or a story that deserves another chapter.

We focus on instruments we’d proudly keep in our own collection, then prepare, inspect, photograph, and present them with the same attention to detail we’d expect when buying for ourselves.

No hype.

No gimmicks.

Just carefully selected instruments for players and collectors who appreciate the details.

item.title: ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle — $1,099
item.description:
$1,099 · Brand New · LV Guitars

ESP LTD Surveyor '87 Electric Bass Rainbow Crackle

The Surveyor '87 delivers a balanced, ergonomic platform with its thin U neck contour and moderate 9 lbs, 12 oz weight — comfortable for extended playing sessions without fatigue. The Rainbow Crackle finish commands visual presence while the bolt-on construction and classic proportions ground this instrument in proven bass fundamentals. Built around vintage-voiced electronics, this bass voices warm, articulate low end suitable for vintage-influenced and contemporary styles alike.

Playability & Build

The 34" scale neck pairs a thin U contour with Macassar ebony fingerboard and 21 XJ frets for smooth, fast navigation across the neck. Neck dimensions measure .760" at the 1st fret and .840" at the 12th fret, establishing a comfortable grip, while the 42mm molded nut and 400mm fingerboard radius support both fingerstyle and pick-driven approaches.

Tone & Electronics

The Seymour Duncan Vintage P and Vintage Jazz pickup pairing delivers warm, full-bodied tone with articulate high-end clarity — classic voicing that cuts through without harshness. Passive electronics with Volume/Balance/Tone controls and Gotoh 201-B4 bridge provide reliable hardware and straightforward tone-shaping, while D'Addario XL165 strings (.045/.065/.085/.105) arrive pre-installed.

Key Features
  • Bolt-on alder body with maple neck and Macassar ebony fingerboard
  • 34" scale length with 42mm molded nut
  • Thin U neck contour; .760" at 1st fret, .840" at 12th fret
  • 21 XJ frets with 400mm fingerboard radius
  • Rainbow Crackle finish with black hardware
  • Seymour Duncan Vintage P (neck) and Vintage Jazz (bridge) pickups
  • Passive electronics: Volume/Balance/Tone layout
  • Gotoh 201-B4 bridge and LTD Vintage tuners
  • D'Addario XL165 strings pre-installed (.045/.065/.085/.105)
  • 9 lbs, 12 oz total weight

The Surveyor '87 combines ergonomic design, vintage-style electronics, and straightforward hardware into a versatile platform suited to players seeking authentic tone and reliable playability.

item.title: Squier Limited Edition Classic Vibe '60s Precision Bass - Aztec Gold — $699.99
item.description:
$699.99 · Excellent · EGA Guitars

🎸 Used – Excellent Condition | Lowest Price Online – Don’t Miss Out!
🚀 Now Offering 2-Day Shipping – Select at Checkout
✨ Every Guitar/Bass Includes a Fresh Set of Strings & a Basic Setup – Read Our Reviews!

🏆 Hassle-Free Returns       

  • New Items: 30 days from delivery
  • Used & Vintage: 7 days from delivery
    Return in original condition for a refund (less shipping). See our full shop policy for details.


Description

Inspired by the legendary instruments of the 1960s, the Limited Edition Classic Vibe™ '60s Precision Bass® captures the timeless look, feel, and unmistakable sound that helped define generations of music. Blending vintage-inspired appointments with modern playability, it delivers an authentic bass experience for players of every level.

A Fender® Designed alnico split single-coil pickup produces the powerful, punchy tone that made the Precision Bass® a studio and stage staple. The comfortable "C"-shaped maple neck, Indian Laurel fingerboard, narrow-tall frets, and vintage-style bridge provide smooth playability and classic feel with dependable performance.

Finished with period-correct styling, including vintage-inspired headstock markings, nickel-plated hardware, and a vintage-tint gloss neck, this Limited Edition Classic Vibe™ '60s Precision Bass® delivers iconic style with legendary Fender-inspired tone.

Item Shown: Limited Edition Classic Vibe™ '60s Precision Bass®, Laurel Fingerboard, Aztec Gold
Model #: 0374510578
Case/Gig Bag: Sold Separately
Weight: 7.7 lbs

Product Specs

Body

  • Body: Poplar
  • Body Finish: Gloss Urethane
  • Body Shape: Precision Bass®
  • Color: Aztec Gold
  • Pickguard: 4-Ply Tortoiseshell

Neck

  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Finish: Tinted Gloss Urethane
  • Neck Shape: "C" Shape
  • Fingerboard: Indian Laurel
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5" (241 mm)
  • Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)
  • Number of Frets: 20
  • Fret Size: Narrow Tall
  • Nut Material: Bone
  • Nut Width: 1.685" (42.8 mm)
  • Position Inlays: White Dot
  • Truss Rod: Head Adjust

Electronics

  • Neck Pickup: Fender® Designed Alnico Split Single-Coil
  • Configuration: Split Single-Coil
  • Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone

Hardware

  • Bridge: 4-Saddle Vintage-Style with Threaded Steel Saddles
  • Tuning Machines: Vintage Style
  • Hardware Finish: Nickel
  • Control Knobs: Knurled Flat-Top
  • Strings: Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105 Gauges)

Miscellaneous

  • Orientation: Right-Hand
  • Series: Classic Vibe

item.title: MTD 535-24 Exotic: Cerejeira 10 Top, Flamed Sycamore Body & Roasted Flamed Maple Fingerboard — $10,400
item.description:
$10,400 · Brand New · FretSpot

MTD 535-24 Exotic: Cerejeira 10 Top, Flamed Sycamore Body & Roasted Flamed Maple Fingerboard

This was the first bass I saw when I walked into the MTD workshop this summer. It did not even have a neck on it yet and I was immediately stopped by that top. I asked Mike if it was available before I even knew what the wood was.

Cerejeira is a Brazilian hardwood I had genuinely never encountered before, and after seeing this one I understand why Mike chose it for a 10 top. The grain curves in and down toward the center of the wood in a way that is unlike anything else in the tonewood world. It has something of the look of burled walnut at first glance but it is not burled, not flamed, not quilted in any conventional sense. It is just its own completely unique visual character, and I have never seen anything quite like it. Color matched wood knobs, matching Cerejeira headstock veneer, and a Purpleheart World MTD logo round out a front elevation that is genuinely one of the most stunning instruments I have had come through FretSpot.

Then flip it over. The body is exotic flamed sycamore with a hand rubbed amber stain, and this bass would be a showpiece without any top at all. The sycamore is extraordinary on its own. The multi-laminate flamed ash and wenge neck with titanium neck bars is one of the rarest neck combinations I have seen from MTD, and the flame running through the ash laminates is just unbelievable. Roasted highly flamed maple fingerboard, matching truss rod cover, Bartolini pickups with magnetic covers standard, Buzz Feiten Tuning System, and custom MTD bridge. Every single wood on this instrument is exceptional. At 9 lbs 7 oz it is a comfortable and well balanced build for how visually loaded it is.

Specs:

  • Model: MTD 535-24 Exotic
  • Body: Flamed Sycamore (Hand Rubbed Amber Stain)
  • Top: Cerejeira 10 Top
  • Neck: Multi-Laminate Flamed Ash & Wenge, Titanium Neck Bars
  • Fingerboard: Roasted Highly Flamed Maple
  • Headstock: Matching Cerejeira Veneer
  • Truss Rod Cover: Matching
  • Knobs: Color Matched to Body
  • Frets: 24 (2-Octave Neck)
  • Scale Length: 35"
  • String Spacing: 19mm
  • Pickups: Bartolini (Magnetic Covers Standard)
  • Bridge: Custom MTD
  • Logo: World MTD (Purpleheart)
  • Tuning System: Buzz Feiten
  • Weight: 9 lbs 7 oz
  • Case: MTD Black Gig Case Included

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Extreme-4 Bass Guitar - See-Thru Black — $500
item.description:
$500 · Excellent · Brian's Gear Shop

Schecter Stiletto Extreme-4 Bass Guitar - See-Thru Black

Like new. Barely used.

FREE SHIPPING!!!


  • Body Shape: Stiletto Extreme Double Cutaway
  • Body Material: Mahogany with Quilted Maple Top
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Profile: Thin C
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Fingerboard Material: Rosewood

  • Electronics: Active 2-Band EQ
  • Controls: Master Volume, Blend, Active EQ (Bass/Treble)
  • Hardware Finish: Black Chrome
  • Tuning Machines: Schecter
  • Finish: See-Thru Black (STBLK)

item.title: '74 RICKENBACKER 4000/4001 MAPLEGLO BASS w/HSC — $2,400
item.description:
$2,400 · Very Good · Four String Michael

EXCEPTIONAL PLAYERS INSTRUMENT. VINTAGE 1974 MAPLEGLO RICKENBACKER 4000/4001S BASS WITH A FANTASTIC CUSTOM G&G HARD SHELL CASE THAT HAS CHENEY HARDWARE AND TWO KEYS.  WAS ORIGINALLY A MODEL 4000 SINGLE PU BASS. PREVIOUS OWNER MODIFIED IT INTO A MODEL 4001S DOUBLE PU. THEY USED SOME NON-RICK PARTS SO I REPLACED THEM ALL USING AUTHENTIC RICKENBACKER PARTS SUCH AS: TOASTER NECK PU - REISSUE HORSESHOE BRIDGE PU MINUS THE "SHOES" - WIRING HARNESS W/ THE VINTAGE TONE PULL/PUSH POT - 60s STYLE PICKGUARD. NECK IS STRAIGHT WITH NEW FRETS. EVERYTHING WORKS FINE. WEIGHS A COMFY 8 3/4 POUNDS. PLAYS, SOUNDS AND LOOKS GREAT.  SELLING THIS INSTRUMENT AS IT IS AND ANYTHING YOU DEEM NECESSARY FROM CHANGING THE STRINGS, NECK ADJUSTMENT, OVERALL SET UP, FRET WORK OR WHATEVER IS UP TO YOU AND AT YOUR EXPENSE. THERE IS NO WARRANTY AND NO RETURN. BY PURCHASING THIS BASS YOU AGREE TO THESE TERMS. ASK QUESTIONS. PLEASE NO OFFERS OR TRADES. CONUS SHIPPING ONLY-NO P.O. BOX OR FREIGHT FORWARDING

item.title: MTD 535-24 with Pale Moon Ebony Top & Fingerboard, Sassafras Body & Flamed Maple Neck — $9,799
item.description:
$9,799 · Brand New · FretSpot

MTD 535-24 with Pale Moon Ebony Top & Fingerboard, Sassafras Body & Flamed Maple Neck

Pale Moon Ebony does not come through FretSpot often, and when it does it never lasts long. Getting pieces of this wood large enough to use for a bass top is genuinely rare, and this MTD 535-24 has it on both the 10 top and the fingerboard. Matching headstock veneer and matching wood knobs with flamed maple underneath tie the whole aesthetic together in a way that is completely cohesive and absolutely stunning. The blonde tones and dark black streaks running through the Pale Moon Ebony have a depth and complexity that photographs cannot fully capture.

I spotted this one during my visit to the MTD workshop this summer and claimed it immediately. Sassafras body with a flamed maple neck running titanium neck bars: this neck is set for life, stable and consistent in any environment. Multiple veneers between the body and the Pale Moon Ebony top add visual complexity at the seams. World MTD logo, custom MTD bridge, matching truss rod cover, Buzz Feiten Tuning System, and Bartolini pickups with magnetic covers standard. At 10 lbs 2 oz it is on the heavier side, but on a wide strap it wears comfortably and the tonal reward is worth it. Ships with the MTD black gig case.

Pale Moon Ebony always sells fast. If you see this on the site it is available. If it is gone someone got there before you.

Specs:

  • Model: MTD 535-24
  • Body: Sassafras
  • Top: Pale Moon Ebony 10 Top
  • Neck: Flamed Maple with Titanium Neck Bars
  • Fingerboard: Pale Moon Ebony
  • Headstock: Matching Pale Moon Ebony Veneer
  • Truss Rod Cover: Matching
  • Knobs: Matching Wood (Flamed Maple)
  • Veneers: Multiple between body and top (2)
  • Frets: 24 (2-Octave Neck)
  • Scale Length: 35"
  • String Spacing: 19mm
  • Pickups: Bartolini (Magnetic Covers Standard)
  • Bridge: Custom MTD
  • Logo: World MTD
  • Tuning System: Buzz Feiten
  • Weight: 10 lbs 2 oz
  • Case: MTD Black Gig Case Included

item.title: MTD 635-24: Flamed Maple Burl Top, Mahogany Body & Birdseye Maple Fingerboard — Serial #2365, Built 2013, Mint Condition — $7,499
item.description:
$7,499 · Excellent · FretSpot

MTD 635-24: Flamed Maple Burl Top, Mahogany Body & Birdseye Maple Fingerboard — Serial #2365, Built 2013, Mint Condition

This MTD 635-24 was built in June of 2013 and it looks like it just left Mike's shop today. Serial number 2365, the 2365th bass of the MTD series, and it is in essentially flawless condition. There is a small mark on the headstock that on closer inspection appears to be figuring in the ebony rather than any actual damage. Beyond that this bass is mint.

The mahogany body with a flamed maple burl top is a combination that produces a genuinely beautiful tonal character. Mahogany delivers rich, warm lower mids that give the instrument its fundamental body and depth. The flamed maple burl top adds a crisp, singing brightness to the voice that sits right on top of that mahogany warmth and keeps every note articulate and present. It is a combination that works especially well on a six string where tonal clarity across a wide range of frequencies matters enormously.

Maple neck, birdseye maple fingerboard, ebony headstock, matching truss rod cover, and Luminlay glow in the dark side dots that charge with UV lighting: a genuinely useful feature for dark stage situations. Rubber knobs, custom Bartolini pickups and 3-band electronics with master volume, pan, treble, mid with 3-position switch selecting 250Hz, 500Hz, or 1000Hz mid points, and bass cut/boost. Buzz Feiten Tuning System for improved intonation working with chords, keyboards, and horns. At 9 lbs 6 oz this is an exceptional weight for a six string bass at this level of quality.

This is a consignment. Trades are not available on this one. Once it sells I cannot source another one exactly like it.

Specs:

  • Model: MTD 635-24
  • Serial Number: 2365060613 (2365th MTD bass built)
  • Date Built: June 6, 2013
  • Body: Mahogany
  • Top: Flamed Maple Burl
  • Neck: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Birdseye Maple
  • Headstock: Ebony
  • Truss Rod Cover: Matching
  • Side Dots: Luminlay (Glow in Dark, UV Activated)
  • Frets: 24
  • Scale Length: 35"
  • Strings: 6
  • Pickups: Custom Bartolini
  • Electronics: Custom Bartolini 3-Band (Master Volume, Pan, Treble, Mid/3-Position Switch, Bass)
  • Mid Switch Positions: 250Hz / 500Hz / 1000Hz
  • Knobs: Rubber
  • Tuning System: Buzz Feiten
  • Weight: 9 lbs 6 oz
  • Condition: Mint (Consignment; no trades available)
  • Case: Gig Bag Included

item.title: Ibanez GWB35-BKF Gary Willis Signature Soundgear Fretless 2004 - Present - Black Flat — $400
item.description:
$400 · Fair · J. Cedric Music

I originally bought this bass with the intention of using it for a significant mod project, but other projects got in the way so it's sat in the case ever since it arrived. As such, I'm copy/pasting the info from the previous listing as this should cover all the relevant info the guitar and nothing has changed:

So I was brave and stupid when I was younger and decided I didn't care for the white fret lines this bass came with. Thus I decided to slice them out and replace them with cedar wood strips which I then polyurethaned over.

Now, the job really didn't come out looking too bad and it still plays just fine, really aside from a few spots up past the 12th that buzz and flat out so the action is a little high to compensate for this. A proper luthier should be able to bring this thing back up to snuff but even as it is, it isn't terrible. There is also some crackling and cutting out at the volume pot. Everything else about this bass should be fine aside from some general scuffs. It really is/was a great bass and it served me well for many gigs. I'll miss it and wish I wasn't such a dope as a kid!

Also comes with pictured hardcase!

item.title: Ibanez SR886-BK Standard Soundgear Bass 1991 - 1992 - Black — $800
item.description:
$800 · Very Good · Schmidt Guitars

For Sale: 1992 Ibanez SR886 6-String Bass – All Black – $800

Classic Japanese-made Ibanez Soundgear! This 1992 SR886 is a rare 6-string from the early ’90s Standard series (produced only 1991-1993). Sleek all-black finish.

Specs:

•  Basswood body, double-cutaway

•  3-piece maple neck, 24-fret rosewood board

•  IBZ J6 pickups with active 2-band EQ

•  Black hardware, Gotoh tuners

•  34” scale, comfortable thin neck

Plays great with clear, articulate tone and strong low B string. Perfect for rock, fusion, prog, or studio use.

Good used condition with normal player wear for its age. Neck straight, frets in good shape, setup and ready to go. Includes original hard case.

$800 OBO. Local pickup preferred or buyer pays shipping.

DM with questions or for more pics!​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

item.title: RKM Janek Gwizdala 5 String 2025 — $3,500
item.description:
$3,500 · Very Good · B’s Bass Stuff

RKM Janek Gwizdala 33” scale Double reverse P 5 string. B-G tuning.

This is number 8 from the first run.  

Holy hell this thing is wonderful. The variety of tones it produces is staggering.  It is in great condition other than one small chip on the bottom of the bass. Only selling it so I can get 6 string version custom made for me.  

I had the nut filed so it is standard five string BEADG tuning currently. I just need that B string.  Good news is that B string is way better than I thought it was going to be on 33” scale. It is clear and crisp. I’m sure it has to do with the carbon fiber reinforced neck . It has a hipshot drop tuner on it so it goes down to A as well. 

It is currently strung with a pretty fresh set of Labella IMA Super Polished string.  

Ships in a Mono Stealth gig bag.

item.title: Squier Vintage Modified Precision Bass PJ 2013 - Lake Placid Blue — $395
item.description:
$395 · Very Good · Ear Friendly

2013 Lake Placid Blue Squier Vintage Modified PJ Bass.

Nice instrument, great sound and cool color combo.

It's in very good condition, with very little play. Nice thin neck, super light weight, great color and Rosewood fretboard. Still has plastic on pickguard.

There might be some bumps, bruises or wear that I was unable to capture in photos, but I tried my best to accurately show the condition of the bass.

Weight: 7.75 lbs.

Sale includes: Bass only.

Selling this bass used, as described. No returns.  

I am careful when packing, quick to ship and all shipments are insured. 

This instrument will need to be setup and adjusted to your tastes/preferences. No under-the-table sales. CONUS shipping only. All orders will be shipped---no local pickups.

Thanks for looking and feel free to message me with any questions. If you liked this instrument, I encourage you to check out my other listings and/or "follow" my shop to keep up with new listings and updates.

item.title: Vox Apache 1B - Cream — $600
item.description:
$600 · Excellent · Todd's Gear Emporium

Fun Bass that has a drum machine built in! One man band… almost…

item.title: Fender American Deluxe Jazz Bass V - Sunburst — $1,350
item.description:
$1,350 · Very Good · Mark1027

Excellent playing bass with normal cosmetic wear from careful use. The most notable cosmetic issue is a finish chip on the lower edge of the body (shown in photos) - happened when my kids were playing nearby one day 😑… Otherwise the instrument is in excellent condition!

Features:
• Made in USA
• 5-string
• Fender American Deluxe series
• Rosewood fingerboard
• Original hardware
• Serial DZ5009731

Strap Locks included.

Everything functions properly.

item.title: Squier Classic Vibe Bass VI 2019 - Present - Black — $450
item.description:
$450 · Excellent · Bill's Gear

This Bass VI has been recently professionally set-up including the tremolo system by German Guitars.

I bought it new. It is much improved over the poor set-up you get when you buy them new.

It is in excellent condition. It has the typical light swirling that comes with any used black bass or guitar. It's minor and it has no chips, scratches or dents whatsoever. You will be getting an instrument that everything works properly on and the same can't be said of brand-new ones - especially the trem system. It includes the trem bar and adjustment tools that came with it. I think it looks great but it's time to thin the herd.

item.title: G&L Tribute Series L-2500 5-String Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 2010s Tobacco Sunburst — $901
item.description:
$901 · Excellent · Tom's Bass Gear

Here we have a pre owned G&L Tribute Series L-2500 5 String Electric Bass.  Beautiful Tobacco Burst finish with matching headstock.  Swamp Ash body, Maple neck with a Rosewood fingerboard.  G&L's saddle lock bridge and MFD humbuckers with Tri Tone control system.  An absolute killer bass with so many capable sounds its staggering. This bass weighs 9lbs 9.7oz.  It comes with a generic wood hard shell case. 

Cosmetically, this bass is in very good to excellent condition.  There is one small finish gank about the size of pen tip. It is right of the foremost mini toggle switch.   The rest of the bass has some light finish marks on the back side.  Nothing major at all.  Truly very clean condition.   

All in all, the G&L Tribute L-2500 is a beast of a bass for the money.  Active/Passic electronics, great sounding pickups, and a beautiful finish.

From G&L's site... 

When Leo Fender launched the G&L L-2000™ bass in 1980, it was clear he was ready to make history all over again. Never before had such a wide range of tones come from a single bass, and today the L-2500™ packs all the features of the latest L-2000 into a 5-string.

Like its L-2000 brother, the foundation of the L-2500 is a pair of G&L Magnetic Field Design™ humbuckers backed by the flexible Tri-Tone™ control system, providing the sort of versatility that’ll let you find your tone no matter what kind of bass line you’re laying down. The Tri-Tone system features a trio of mini toggles for pickup selection, series/parallel signal routing and active/passive operation, as well as a trio of knobs for volume, treble and bass. With all that under the hood, the L-2500 brings the kind of chops that’ll fit in any situation, from classic R&B to power-packed rock to poppin’ funk. What’s more, the L-2500 features an innovative Leo Fender-designed Saddle-Lock™ bridge to transfer string energy right into the body end-grain for an incredibly resonant instrument.

The Tribute® Series L-2500 delivers the magic of Leo’s revolutionary design at a price attainable for every working musician. Once you play a G&L Tribute Series, you’ll never look back.

item.title: Squier FSR Classic Vibe '60s Precision Bass with Matching Headstock 2021 - Sonic Blue — $295
item.description:
$295 · Very Good · Ear Friendly

Sonic Blue Squier Classic Vibe Precision Bass with matching headstock.

Nice instrument, great sound and cool color combo. The fretboard on this bass is extraordinary. Beautiful grains---looks fantastic against the Sonic Blue body and matching headstock.

It's in very good condition, with very little play. That said there are some minor blemishes that I've tried to accurately show the condition in photographs.

Sale includes: Bass only.

Selling this guitar used, as described. No returns.

I am careful when packing, quick to ship and all shipments are insured. 

Instruments will probably need to be setup and adjusted to your tastes/preferences. No under-the-table sales. CONUS shipping only. All orders will be shipped---no local pickups.

Thanks for looking and feel free to message me with any questions. If you liked this instrument, I encourage you to check out my other listings and/or "follow" my shop to keep up with new listings and updates.

item.title: Fender Justin Meldal-Johnsen Road Worn Signature Mustang Bass 2018 - Present - Faded Daphne Blue — $1,399
item.description:
$1,399 · Excellent · Kurtz Gear

This Fender Mustang bass is in excellent condition. Frets are in great shape. Includes a Fender gig bag. Currently strung up with rounds but I had a set of flat wounds on it which will be included as well. Incredible playing and feeling bass. I’m a guitar player that purchased this bass to play around with but just don’t have the time to get any play.

item.title: Gibson Eb bass - Black — $40
item.description:
$40 · Very Good · David's Shop

Gibson bass pickup.

Used in the new T-bird basses and Eb basses. Neck pickup

item.title: DiMarzio Model One DP120 and DP145 2020 - Black — $95
item.description:
$95 · Very Good · David's Shop

2 DiMarzio Model One pickups.

The two different pole spacings to fit either neck or bridge.

Were used on a Gibson SG bass. Neck and bridge.

Sell as a pair.

item.title: Cort Artisan Space 4 - Active Headless Bass — $589
item.description:
$589 · Excellent · Cooch's Guitar & Bass

The Cort Space 4 Artisan headless electric bass combines a cutting-edge design with expert craftsmanship. It's light in weight and extremely well balanced (no headstock, no neck dive). The tuning system is so simple you'll look at it and wonder why you didn't think of it. It takes good old std bass strings, not those double ball end strings like Steinberger uses. Hard to find and very expensive to buy. It comes with two Bartolini MK1 pickups with an active eq. The bass can be also played passive by pulling up on the volume control. The eq is very effective. The range of bass tones available are most impressive for a bass in this price range. If you slap you found your bass. The neck is a seven piece laminate made from Roasted Maple, Walnut, and Purple heart with a Roasted Maple fingerboard. It is extremely stable. There is no fret buzz and no deadspots This bass has sat for weeks on its stand and still be perfectly in tune. This bass is as close to brand new as you'll find anywhere. If I told you it was brand new you I promise you would believe me. It's really never been played.

I've come to the realization that I have to many basses to ever play them all. I like the bass a lot but it was an impulse buy for me and it just doesn't get played. I own 5 other basses. Time to thin the herd. Maybe even a couple of others to go with it.

It will come with a brand new never used Cort gig bag made for the bass.

item.title: Bogart Classic V headless 5 string bass graphite neck — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Excellent · David's G.A.S. Works

used Blackstone Classic V headless bass in excellent condition. Comes with padded gig bag that came with it, one of the clips that secured one of the carrying straps has broken - I replaced with a carabiner which does the job nicely. I have a big collection of headless basses, mostly Statii, trying to get down to a reasonable size which is why this and others are up for sale.

•Origin: Germany

•Body Material: Composite body with exotic wood facing

•Colour: Natural

•Finish: Satin

•Neck Finish: Satin

•Neck Material: Carbon fibre

•Fingerboard: Phenolic resin

•Inlays: Side dots

•Pickups: Bartolini

•Electronics: Bartolini

•Controls: Volume, pickup pan, bass, middle, treble, tone, active passive switch, mid switch

•Hardware: Black

•Gig Bag/Case: gig bag

•Frets: 24

•String Spacing: 18mm

•Nut Width: 51mm

•Scale Length: 34”

•Weight: 9.1lb

item.title: Lakland Skyline 55-02 Custom with Delano Pickups — $1,400
item.description:
$1,400 · Excellent · Robert's Shop

I am the original owner of this bass brand new which was purchased from Sweetwater on August 20, 2024. The only modifications made to this bass is the added Custom Black Pearloid Pickguard and Delano Pickups. Everything else is standard with this model bass. The bass plays great. I'm just interested in purchasing some other gear.

Lakland 55-02 Skyline Custom Series 5-string Electric Bass Features:

  • Comfort-contoured ash body; polyurethane finish
  • Flat-sawn maple neck with graphite reinforcement bars; satin urethane finish
  • Ebony fingerboard, 10–13" compound radius, block inlays
  • Lakland MM-J configuration pickups
  • Master Volume (push/pull for preamp bypass)
  • Pickup Blend control
  • Bass Cut/Boost
  • Midrange Cut/Boost
  • Treble Cut/Boost
  • 3-way bridge pickup coil split toggle
  • 35" scale length, 22 frets
  • Plek Pro fret treatment for optimized playability
  • Lakland Dual Access bridge, .75" string spacing
  • 1.81" plastic nut
  • Hipshot Ultralight tuners
  • 5/32" truss rod, neck heel access

item.title: Warrior Artist 03 - Natural — $6,000
item.description:
$6,000 · Excellent · Music City Music

A Warrior 5 string Custom Artist bass made for Teddy Gentry of the country music supergroup "Alabama." This instrument is one of a kind with mother of pearl inlays, ebony finger board, black bridge and tuners with the Bartolini pick up system. This is an incredible instrument that plays and sounds amazing. A player's bass with incredible tone. Comes with original locking hard shell case and all the original case candy - including an ostrich skin strap with strap locks.

item.title: Elrick EVO Gold 4: Quilted Ambrosia Maple Top, Birdseye Maple Fingerboard & Bartolini 3-Band — Mint Condition, 7 lbs 11 oz — $4,199
item.description:
$4,199 · Excellent · FretSpot

Elrick EVO Gold 4: Quilted Ambrosia Maple Top, Birdseye Maple Fingerboard & Bartolini 3-Band — Mint Condition, 7 lbs 11 oz

Rob Elrick has been building some of the finest handcrafted boutique basses in America out of his Chicago shop for decades, and the EVO Gold is his flagship bolt-on design: a heel-less body contour that gives you unrestricted access all the way up the neck, meticulous attention to wood selection, and electronics chosen specifically to enhance the natural voice of the instrument rather than mask it.

This example came in used and in mint condition, and the first thing worth knowing is how it plays. Super low action, zero buzzing anywhere on the neck, and an effortless feel from the first fret to the last. At 7 lbs 11 oz it is one of the lightest four string basses at this quality level you will find anywhere. It is the kind of bass you strap on and immediately forget you are wearing.

The Quilted Ambrosia Maple top in natural satin is visually stunning: complex, organic figuring with the characteristic dark mineral streaks that make Ambrosia Maple so distinctive and sought after. Red Heart Maple neck with dual carbon rod reinforcement keeps the neck stable and consistent in any environment. Birdseye maple fingerboard with birdseye maple block inlays, Luminlay side dots, 16" radius, and a zero fret for perfectly consistent open string intonation. Bartolini dual coil pickups at neck and bridge feeding the Bartolini 3-band preamp with a 3-position mid switch and active/passive switching. Elrick by Hipshot bridge at 19mm string spacing, Hipshot Ultralite tuners, and Dunlop strap buttons. Handcrafted in Chicago, Illinois.

Specs:

  • Model: Elrick EVO Gold 4
  • Construction: Bolt-On
  • Body: Alder
  • Top: Quilted Ambrosia Maple
  • Finish: Natural Satin
  • Neck: Red Heart Maple (Dual Carbon Rod Reinforcement, Satin Finish)
  • Fingerboard: Birdseye Maple (16" Radius)
  • Inlays: Birdseye Maple Blocks
  • Side Dots: Luminlay
  • Nut: Corian
  • Frets: 24 Plus Zero Fret, Nickel
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • String Spacing: 19mm
  • Pickups: Bartolini Dual Coil (Neck & Bridge)
  • Electronics: Bartolini 3-Band
  • Controls: Volume, Balance, Bass, Mid (3-Position Switch), Treble, Active/Passive Switch
  • Bridge: Elrick by Hipshot
  • Tuners: Hipshot Ultralite
  • Strap Buttons: Dunlop Dual Design
  • Hardware: Black
  • Weight: 7 lbs 11 oz
  • Condition: Mint (Used)
  • Case: Elrick Logo Backpack Gig Bag Included

item.title: Hofner 500/1 V62 Reissue Left Handed - Sunburst — $3,719.41
item.description:
$3,719.41 · Very Good · Papercraft Records Gear Shop

Höfner Beatles Bass Left Handed


In very good condition. Almost mint.


Works perfectly.

*The only modification is my Luthier added a Schaller Strap Lock.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4003 Left-Handed - Fireglo — $3,719.41
item.description:
$3,719.41 · Very Good · Papercraft Records Gear Shop

Rickenbacker 4003 Fireglo Left Handed


In pretty good condition almost mint.

Works perfectly.

item.title: Roscoe Century 5 Standard Plus: Matte Tobacco Sunburst, Swamp Ash, Aguilar PJ — Keith Roscoe Certified Pre-Owned with Full Warranty — $3,299
item.description:
$3,299 · Excellent · FretSpot

Roscoe Century 5 Standard Plus: Matte Tobacco Sunburst, Swamp Ash, Aguilar PJ — Keith Roscoe Certified Pre-Owned with Full Warranty

A good customer had some life circumstances and this Century 5 Standard Plus came back to Keith Roscoe. He went through the entire instrument top to bottom: restrung it, reset the setup, full inspection. Full warranty. Essentially a new bass at about $1,000 off the new price. That is a genuinely exceptional value on a USA-made Roscoe.

Swamp Ash body in a matte tobacco sunburst finish: warm, classic, and immediately beautiful. The matte finish is worth calling out specifically because it changes the feel of the instrument in a meaningful way. This is a lighter finish that lets you feel the actual wood grain under your hand on the back of the neck and body. More organic, more natural, more tactile than a gloss instrument. It gives this bass a character that a lot of players specifically seek out and it fits the vibe of the tobacco sunburst perfectly.

Maple neck and maple fingerboard, Aguilar PJ pickups with the Aguilar OBP-2 active/passive preamp, volume, pan, bass/treble, and passive tone controls. Chrome hardware throughout. Condition is excellent with two very minor imperfections: a tiny nick on the body and a small mark on the headstock. Both are consistent with the organic character of a matte finish instrument and take nothing away from playability or tone.

At 7 lbs 14 oz on swamp ash this is one of the lightest five string basses I have had come through FretSpot at this level of quality. Full Keith Roscoe warranty, certified and inspected, ships with gig bag.

Specs:

  • Model: Roscoe Century 5 Standard Plus
  • Body: Swamp Ash
  • Finish: Matte Tobacco Sunburst
  • Neck: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Maple
  • Scale Length: 35"
  • Strings: 5
  • Pickups: Aguilar PJ Set
  • Preamp: Aguilar OBP-2 (Active/Passive)
  • Controls: Volume, Pan, Bass/Treble, Passive Tone
  • Hardware: Chrome
  • Weight: 7 lbs 14 oz
  • Condition: Excellent (Minor Imperfections as Noted; Keith Roscoe Re-Certified, Full Warranty)
  • Case: Gig Bag Included

item.title: 2024 Fodera Emperor Standard 6 Special — $5,300
item.description:
$5,300 · Excellent · Rebecca's Gear Bazaar

Up for sale is a 2024 Fodera Emperor 6 Standard Special. It has an Ash body and Curly Walnut top with the classic Fodera playability.


This is a lightweight, extremely resonant instrument with a warm, articulate voice that sits perfectly in a mix. Beautiful craftsmanship and sound in pristine condition.


Specs:


Model: Emperor 6 Standard Special

Strings: 6

Scale Length: 34”

Body: Ash

Top Wood: Walnut Curly

Neck: 3-piece Maple (Standard profile)

Fingerboard: Maple Birdseye

Frets: 24 (Large)

Inlays: Black Dots

Neck Joint: Bolt-on

Finish: Satin Clear

Headstock: Modern 6 (3+3)

Hardware & Electronics


Tuners: Hipshot Ultralites

Bridge: 6-string, 17.5mm spacing

Nut: Brass

Pickups: Fodera / Duncan Dual Coils

Preamp: Fodera Standard, 3-band

Controls: Standard layout

Strings Installed: 34-45-65-85-105-125 (Nickel Fodera DRx)

Condition


Excellent condition overall

No structural issues or repairs

Frets are Plek'ed and clean with plenty of life

Never left my studio


Includes


Fodera ATA hard case (Reg/Long)

Tools and paperwork

Original Fodera shipping box


This bass delivers the classic Emperor feel with air and bloom and a clear B string, piano-like highs, and a tight but warm low end. Perfect for modern gospel, fusion, studio work, or anyone wanting a versatile high-end 6.

item.title: Peavey Foundation sunburst — $600
item.description:
$600 · Excellent · Josh's Gear Locker

Very nice sunburst foundation shows that it has been played but excellent for its age in a nice peavey case

item.title: G & L JB Placentia Series Jazz Bass 2021 - Cream White — $385
item.description:
$385 · Excellent · Glenn's Gear Emporium

G&L Placentia Jazz Bass

  • Brand
  • G&L

Model

  • Placentia JB

Finish

  • Vintage White

Year

  • 2022 to 2024

Top Material

  • Poplar

String Type

  • Steel

Frets

Right / Left Handed

  • Right Handed

Fretboard Material

Fretboard Radius

Neck Material

Body Shape

Body Material

Number of Strings

Pickup Configuration

Scale Length

  • 34"
  • The G&L Placentia JB electric bass features a poplar body, maple fingerboard and dual JB pickups.This JB projects a time-honored and subtly progressive vibe that embraces the past while also being unafraid of moving forward. The slim, hard rock maple neck features a classic gloss finish, while its comfortable 9" radius and medium-jumbo nickel frets deliver modern playability. What’s more, the JB features an innovative Leo Fender-designed Saddle-Lock bridge to transfer string energy right into the body end-grain for an incredibly resonant instrument. Once you play a G&L Tribute Series, you’ll never look back.

item.title: Fender Contemporary Precision Bass Lyte MIJ 1989 - 1995 - Frost White — $900
item.description:
$900 · Very Good · Josh's Gear Locker

Nice fender precision lyre has dings here and there nothing major a great player in a gator zip up case

item.title: Fender 1978 Fretless Precision Bass - Rosewood Fingerboard - Mocha Brown — $2,750
item.description:
$2,750 · Very Good · Branson’s Basses

Offering up this beautiful vintage Fender ‘78 Fretless P-Bass in Mocha.

Still paired with it’s original hardshell case that has protected it for 48 years.

Sounds just wonderful, and plays effortlessly with nice low action. Beautiful dark Rosewood fretboard. Rear of the neck is in fine shape. Body has a couple finish chips that reveal the sealer clearcoat underneath. Still has it’s original bridge cover and thumb-rest.

item.title: Yamaha TRBX605FM 5-String 2024 - Present - Indigo Blue — $700
item.description:
$700 · Excellent · Josh's Music Mart

This is a literally brand new bass. It has only been opened and tuned and checked out. Comes with original box and all accessories. My friend is finally getting rid of a bass I have wanted for a long time so I am selling this one to buy his. This bass is great and has tons of tonal options, looks gorgeous too. CASE NOT INCLUDED

item.title: Peavey T-45 1980s - Sunburst — $800
item.description:
$800 · Excellent · Josh's Gear Locker

Great condition for its age definitely needs a new set of strings. The case on the other hand isnt the best but doing its job.

item.title: Aria Pro II SB-1200 / SB-1000 P/J Active Neck-Through Bass MIJ Black — $1,095
item.description:
$1,095 · Very Good · Lost Signal Instrument Co.

Up for sale is a rare Aria Pro II SB-series active P/J neck-through bass in transparent black finish. Based on its construction and features, I believe this is most likely a late-1980s / early-1990s SB-1200 or late SB-1000 P/J variant.

This bass features neck-through construction, ash or sen body wings, a five-piece maple neck, rosewood fingerboard, matching transparent black headstock, rosewood truss rod cover, gold hardware, and the recessed/countersunk bridge seen on higher-end Aria SB models from this era. The pickups are active Aria Pro II-branded Precision/Jazz style pickups with a simple and usable control layout: master volume, master tone, and a 3-way pickup selector.

The bass weighs 9.3 lbs and has a comfortable neck profile that falls between a Jazz Bass and Precision Bass feel. Measurements are approximately 1.58” nut width, 2.15” width at the 12th fret, 0.92” neck thickness at the 1st fret, and 1.03” thickness at the 12th fret.

Tonally, this is not a typical passive Fender-style bass. It has a more modern, aggressive active sound, closer to a Spector or EMG-loaded bass, while still retaining the feel and sustain of a Japanese neck-through instrument.

The bass has been set up, the frets are in good shape with plenty of life left, and all electronics are working. There are many light scratches and buckle marks on the back, along with normal age-related wear to the gold hardware, but there are no structural issues noted. A very cool and uncommon Aria Pro II SB for players or collectors looking for something outside the usual single-pickup SB-1000 format.

item.title: Lakland Skyline DJ-5 Darryl Jones Signature with Maple Fretboard 2002 - Present - Natural — $1,400
item.description:
$1,400 · Very Good · Daniel's Shop

Lakland skyline DJ-5 in very good condition. This bass has LowEnd jazz pickups installed as well as an active/passive toggle switch and mid freq. toggle switch. Knobs for master volume, high, low, and mid. 35” scale. One other upgrade is the brass nut. I was using this bass for a project and decided on something else. I tried a couple different gauges and tunings so it may require a small set up for your strings of choice.


Let me know if you have questions, thanks for looking.

item.title: Tokai Jazz Sound 1982 - Natural — $800
item.description:
$800 · Fair · Gear of Wilson

old tokai jazz bass. it was professionally refinished to a natural finish, with a few little spots intentionally left over from the previous finish (black). action is a bit high

item.title: 1973 Fender USA Vintage Jazz Bass Pickup — $150
item.description:
$150 · Non Functioning · JJ's Gear Garage

1973 Fender USA Vintage Jazz Bass Pickup NON-WORKING. Grey Bottom with original cover, staggered polepieces and flat head screws. No reading on my Fluke multimeter. Dead, Will be packed well, fully insured. PLEASE ASK ANY QUESTIONS BEFORE BUYING OR MAKING AN OFFER.

48 STATES ONLY, NO OVERSEAS

item.title: Fender Contemporary Precision Bass Lyte MIJ 1989 - 1994 - Violin Burst — $900
item.description:
$900 · Excellent · Josh's Gear Locker

Great condition, Fender precision lyte only issues it has the gold is fading and minor scuffs from use

item.title: Handmade Fender Musicmaster Style Bass 2025 - Satin clear — $450
item.description:
$450 · Very Good · John's Gear Outlet

I built this bass last summer. The body is a piece of cherry that I cut and shaped by hand. The neck is a Fender licensed short scale bass neck. Tuners are genuine Fender, bridge and electronics are from a Squier Bronco Bass. It needs a little bit of work to get the action just right, and the body has a couple of uneven spots, but all in all a great short scale bass. I have light Ernie Ball flatlands on it and it plays great!

Feel free to make an offer!

item.title: Ibanez Musician MC-924H AJ10 Alphonso Johnson Signature, Factory Hybrid Fretted & Fretless Bass, 1981 - Dark Satin — $4,292.81
item.description:
$4,292.81 · Very Good · Jan Bass Collection

Up for sale, a 1981 Ibanez Musician MC-924H Hybrid half-fretless bass in excellent, 100% original condition and in perfect working order. Originally designed as a signature model for Alphonso Johnson under the AJ10 designation, exceedingly few of these basses are known to exist from their limited 1979-83 production window. Alphonso Johnson is perhaps best known for his ‘70s stint with Weather Report, as well as for recording and performing with artists like Phil Collins and the Grateful Dead.

Tonewoods include five-piece maple/walnut neck-through construction with dark stained ash body wings on a mahogany core and, most unique of all, a slab ebony fretboard which is fretted up to the 12th fret and fretless after the octave. Weighing 10lbs 2oz, this bass has healthy natural sustain and thick low end presence, offering both ample growl and broad thump. The slab ebony fretboard ensures ideal note attack and articulation, and the Super 4 pickups deliver a sound that's both commanding and detailed, governed by switchable active and passive circuitry. The MC-924H has been professionally setup here at Mike & Mike’s Guitar Bar with D’Addario Chromes 45-105 flatwound strings, and easy-playing action, and accurate intonation.

The three-piece maple neck is reinforced with strips of walnut and sports a medium C-shaped profile carve with ample shoulder and lightly rolled fretboard edges, measuring .825” deep at the 1st fret and .935” at the 12th. The slab ebony fretboard retains the original flawless slender fretwire up to the octave. There is light string wear into the ebony fretboard beyond fret 12, and the board extends all the way to a second octave beneath the D and G strings. This bass plays cleanly up the 34” scale with a straight neck and a responsive, optimally-adjusted truss rod. The nut measures 1.650” (42mm) in width, crafted from brass for optimal articulation and sustain. The headstock has a dark stained ash veneer and retains the original gold “Musician” truss rod cover. The VelveTune B II gold tuning machines (as the name would suggest) turn smoothly and hold pitch well. On the back of the headstock, the gold “Crafted in Japan” script is intact and the G81-prefix serial number dates this bass to July, 1981.

The electronics work exactly as intended and are 100% stock, with both Super 4 single coils wired to a three-way pickup selector, Master Volume, passive Tone, center detent Boost/Cut, and Treble, Mid, and Bass knobs, along with a two-way mini toggle switch that activates the preamp/three-band EQ. Plastics comprise the original knobs, which have ridged grips, and the gold hardware is notably clean, including the Accu-cast B toploader bridge and saddles.

The gloss finish is abbreviated as "DS" in the Ibanez catalog for Dark Stain, although this is a non-catalog offering. Cosmetic wear on the finish includes an area of targeted buckle rash on the back at the bass-side body horn, light finish wear on the lower bout body edge, and a number of minor dings and faint finish scratches on the body as a whole, largely concentrated along the perimeter. The smooth gloss on the neck profile highlights birdseye figuring in the maple and feels fast in the palm.

item.title: Kramer XL 8 string bass 1980 Red — $4,770.06
item.description:
$4,770.06 · Very Good · Jan Bass Collection

1980 Kramer XL-8
This bass is in exceptional condition and completely stock. Neck is great and electronics are fully functional.  
Bass in case weighs in at around 30 lbs. thus the shipping.

  • Body: Maple w/ Walnut Stripes
  • Fingerboard: 24 Fret Ebonol 
  • Neck: Aluminum Bolt-On
  • Pickups: Dimarzio (stock)
  • Bridge: Leo Quan Badass
  • Comes with Original HSC

item.title: Warrior Soldier 1997 - Zebra wood / birdseye maple — $2,260.70
item.description:
$2,260.70 · Very Good · James's Gear Outlet

*Please note one machine head has been replaced with a matching Gotoh machine head.

1997 Warrior Instruments Soldier 4-String Bass – Zebrawood / Birdseye Maple (Serial #9702129)OverviewUp for sale is an incredible piece of boutique American bass history: a 1997 Warrior Soldier 4-string bass (Serial #9702129). Hand-carved during the golden early era of Warrior Instruments in Rossville, Georgia, this bass represents the pinnacle of their custom-shop craftsmanship.As the 129th instrument ever completed by the shop, it was hand-built in February 1997 back when Warrior was a tiny, family-run operation. Early Warrior instruments from this era are highly sought after by collectors for their intense attention to detail, premium wood selection, and unmatched ergonomics before any part of their production process became standardized.The Tonewoods & BuildWhile featuring a comfortable, punchy bolt-on neck design, this instrument features the signature visual aesthetic of a neck-through bass.

  • Body Wings: Stunning, deeply figured solid Zebrawood with striking vertical grain patterns.
  • Neck Center-Strip: A multi-laminate accent strip running straight through the center of the body.
  • Fingerboard: A premium, highly figured Birdseye Maple fretboard that adds beautiful visual contrast and a bright, articulate snap to the overall tone.

Electronics & Hardware

  • Pickup: A single, massive premium Bartolini Soapbar pickup positioned perfectly in the sweet spot.
  • Controls: Clean and intuitive 3-knob layout (Volume, Tone, and Preamp control).
  • Hardware: High-end gold top-loading bridge and matching gold tuning pegs that perfectly complement the natural, organic wood tones.

Specifications

  • Brand: Warrior Instruments
  • Model: Soldier 4-String
  • Year: 1997 (February)
  • Serial Number: 9702129 (Production #129)
  • Country of Origin: USA
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Strings  4

Ships securely packed in hard shell case.

Shipping available across Canada and the USA. 

Buyer pays shipping. Please message with postal/zip code for an exact quote.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Bongo Bass 6 String - Tangerine Pearl — $2,275
item.description:
$2,275 · Excellent · The Music City Collection

This rarely played Ernie Ball Music Man Bongo 6 HH is a beautifully balanced electric bass with a comfortable, resonant basswood body and a fast-playing maple neck with a rosewood fingerboard. A generous double cutaway design gives you effortless access to the upper register, while two powerful humbuckers with neodymium magnets deliver boatloads of beefy tone. Ernie Ball states that you've also got a piezo bridge pickup, which bassists love using to add an extra dimension to the humbucker tone.Not sure, because this seller never used it. There is an onboard 18-volt preamp with a 4-band EQ completes the fabulous Ernie Ball Music Man Bongo 6 HH . Rare discontinued Tangerine Pearl.

NO CASE INCLUDED . Bass will be professionally packaged protected with an excessive amount of bubble wrap. Offering a very affordable shipping charge will this seller paying the balance of the shipping charge.

This bass has never been played out of my studio. Its way too big for this girl to play. But it is an amazing and versatile 6 string bass. I bought it from Guitar Center and they did not have the original case which didn't matter to me because I never planned on playing it out on a gig.

item.title: Univox Violin Bass Model 120 1969 about - black — $825
item.description:
$825 · Good · Kim's Gear Outlet

I took this to Gryphon Music Store and they went through it to make sure everything worked. They found a couple of loose wires and fixed them. I also purchased a gig bag for it. It will need strings. It has strings, but some of them were not the correct size and Gryphon put on some they had around.

item.title: Vintage 1980s Cort Action P-Bass MIK Brownburst Grabber/Ripper Style — $400
item.description:
$400 · Excellent · Lost Signal Instrument Co.

Up for sale is a cool and unusual 1980s Cort one-pickup bass, most likely an early Korean-made Cort Action Bass or related model. These early Cort basses are not common, and this one has a great vintage import look: rounded double-cut body, brown-burst woodgrain finish, book-matched veneer top and back, faux gold center stripes, matching headstock, rosewood fingerboard, and a simple passive control layout.

The design has a definite late-1970s/early-1980s vibe, somewhere between a Gibson Grabber/Ripper, an Aria/Westone import bass, and an early Cort original shape. It has a very practical setup: one volume, one tone, and a single P-style pickup placed in a sweet spot that gives it a punchy, full, straightforward bass tone.

The bass weighs 8.4 lbs, has a 1.65” nut width, and has a comfortable modern P-bass style neck profile. It is a full 34” scale bass, but because the body carries the weight so well, it balances beautifully and feels easier to play than many long-scale basses. There is no neck dive.

The bass is fully functional, the truss rod works, the pickup and controls work properly, and it is currently set up with low action. The frets have about 80% life remaining. Cosmetically, it is very clean for a 1980s instrument, with only light signs of use.

A great choice for someone looking for a vintage Korean-made Cort, a lightweight passive P-style bass, or something with the look and feel of a cool old Grabber/Ripper-style import without the Gibson price tag.
